New Platform Rollout — US Coupons

Phased migration from legacy to new merchant template · Phase 2 live — 3,524 merchants · April 28, 2026

Last refresh: 2026-04-28 09:34 CET

Apr 27 finding: 5 of 6 flagged were healthy on direct traffic — email/scanner artifacts, not page bugs.
Bottom line: 50/50 case for ramp is strong. 6 merchants flagged by auto-rule but Apr 27 PostHog cohort-wide validation showed 5 of 6 were false positives (email/scanner traffic, not page bugs) — see deep-dive card. Earliest ramp window: need 7 consecutive days +5pp in 33/67 + utm-segmented Tableau view from Veronika. Source: Tableau — New Platform Dashboard ↗

⚠️ 6 merchants flagged by auto-rule — needs PostHog confirm auto-detected · last 7 days · downgraded from P0 after Apr 27 PostHog validation

Apr 27 finding — these are likely false positives. Cohort-wide PostHog cross-check: 5 of 6 merchants flagged by this rule had healthy 45-79% direct-traffic CTR on new platform. Aggregate CTR was dragged below 15% by email-attributed traffic (mostly bot/email-security-gateway URL scanners with $direct referrer, 35s sessions, 0 clicks). The rule fires on email-blast days, not on real page bugs. Do NOT file Jira tickets from this list without PostHog direct-traffic confirmation — see deep-dive card below.

Detection rule: New CTR < 15% AND Old CTR > 30% AND New UV ≥ 100. Stratified into chronic (multi-day) vs single-day anomaly (one-shot drop). Both buckets need PostHog direct-traffic confirm before treating as bugs.

🔴 Chronic — 5 merchants broken across multiple days

Multiple low-CTR days OR >50% of recent days below threshold. These are systemic and need eng investigation.

Merchant Worst day New CTR Old CTR UV Pattern
Keeper Security [33/67] Apr 24 0.0% 33.3% 189 new / 6 old Chronic — most days broken
Kay Jewelers [33/67] Apr 22 4.7% 43.4% 106 new / 53 old Chronic — most days broken · 2 bad days last 7
Chime [33/67] Apr 22 5.1% 36.8% 157 new / 68 old Chronic — multiple low-CTR days in last 7 · 5 bad days last 7
Zales [33/67] Apr 23 7.1% 37.9% 593 new / 625 old Chronic — most days broken
Sixt Car Rental [33/67] Apr 24 11.5% 51.6% 191 new / 64 old Chronic — most days broken

🟠 Single-day anomalies — 1 merchant with one-shot CTR drop

Only one bad day in the window. Likely email-blast or scanner-traffic artifacts (Apr 27 PostHog finding: email-Desktop $direct = bot/scanner, ~5% CTR with no clicks). Do not treat as page bugs without PostHog confirmation. See deep-dive below.

Merchant Worst day New CTR Old CTR UV Pattern
1-800 Contacts [50/50] Apr 23 7.3% 36.2% 287 new / 58 old Single-day anomaly — UV spike 5.5× normal (likely email-blast / scanner artifact)
Verification ladder before any Jira ticket:
  1. Confirm direct-traffic CTR is unhealthy on new platform. Run PostHog HogQL: SELECT countIf($pageview)/countIf($autocapture matching 'Get Code|Get Deal|Get Reward'…) with utm_medium null/empty for the merchant URL. If direct CTR is >30%, the rule fired on email/scanner traffic — skip ticket.
  2. Check if scanner/bot traffic dominates. If email-Desktop $direct sessions with <60s and 0 clicks are >50% of pageviews, it's URL scanner traffic, not a bug.
  3. Only if both checks fail (real users, real low CTR): file Jira bug, tag Richard, link PostHog session replays.
  4. Pipeline upgrade pending: wire in Florence's 407 legacy-protected list to skip those merchants from detection entirely; wire in utm-segmented Tableau view (assigned to Veronika) for source-stratified CTR.

Top 50/50 takeaways:
  • 22/41 merchants positive in recent window.
  • 6 to investigate now (recent Δ < -3pp AND ≥200 UV): The Home Depot (-19.3pp · 6,746 UV), Expedia (-15.6pp · 921 UV), Hotels.com (-11.8pp · 877 UV), 1-800 Contacts (-8.3pp · 212 UV), Verizon Wireless (-6.8pp · 292 UV)...
  • Fresh regressions to watch: Verizon Wireless (clean +1.0 → recent -6.8), Edible Arrangements (clean +7.0 → recent -5.2), Tripadvisor (clean +7.5 → recent -2.1).... Flipped from positive to negative.

What we know:
  1. Apr 24 inversion — first day Old beat New (45.0% vs 44.0%). See P0 broken merchants card above for the merchant-level cause.
  2. Pattern is traffic-spike dependent. Broken merchants look fine on low-volume days. When external traffic (SEM/affiliate/email) sends users to the new merchant page, the conversion path breaks.
  3. Routing drift unchanged: 33/67 actual split running ~40/60 (target 33/67). Stable but unexplained.
Ramp-decision criteria: need 7 consecutive days ≥+5pp before recommending 33→50% ramp. Currently last day = +3.9pp. Counter reset due to recent inversion.

📊 Insights & signals — what to take seriously

✅ Working
  • 50/50 case for ramp is solid. +6.0pp lift across 11 days, 11/11 days won.
  • Top-volume merchants are crushing it — see the 50/50 table above for ⭐ winners.
  • Attribution fixes held: Mar 25-Apr 14 contamination period is over. Apr 16+ data is the canonical read.
🔴 Broken
  • 6 merchants flagged by auto-rule — but Apr 27 PostHog cohort-wide validation showed 5 of 6 were false positives (healthy direct CTR, dragged by email/scanner traffic). Don't treat as P0 page bugs without PostHog confirm.
  • 33/67 had recent inversion days in long-tail cohort. Resets the 7-day-clean ramp counter.
🟠 Watch closely
  • Verizon Wireless: was +1.0pp clean → -6.8pp recent. Fresh regression on previously winning merchant.
  • Edible Arrangements: was +7.0pp clean → -5.2pp recent. Fresh regression on previously winning merchant.
  • Tripadvisor: was +7.5pp clean → -2.1pp recent. Fresh regression on previously winning merchant.
  • The Home Depot (high volume): -19.3pp on 6,746 UV. Largest absolute click impact.
🟣 Open questions
  • Routing drift: 33/67 actual split running ~40/60 (target 33/67). Richard ownership.
  • Email-flow on new platform: Apr 27 PostHog cohort-wide finding — email CTR 5-25% vs direct 45-79%, dragging aggregates. Email-Desktop $direct (3,379 sessions, 35s, 0.07 CTAs) is bot/scanner profile. Needs (a) email-flow audit on new platform, (b) bot filter on PostHog/Tableau, (c) utm-segmented Tableau view.
  • Direct-only CTR drop Wk1→Wk2 (~10pp): 50/50 cohort direct CTR fell ~40% → ~30% from Apr 14-19 to Apr 21-27. Not explained by traffic-mix. Check deploys Apr 19-20 with Richard.
  • commission_usd null in rep.new_platform_dashboard for 50/50 + 33/67 cohorts — blocks RPV cross-check. Mark/BI follow-up.

✅ Action plan

If direct CTR ≥ 30%, no Jira ticket — it's email/scanner traffic dragging aggregate. Apr 27 cohort-wide check: 5 of 6 were false positives. Martin Today

🔬 PostHog merchant-page deep-dive Apr 13–27 · 50/50 cohort · new platform only · external to auto-pipeline

Three findings reframe the auto-detected "broken merchants": (1) the rule fires heavily on email-blast days — 5 of 6 flagged merchants have healthy 45-79% direct-traffic CTR; (2) email-Desktop $direct is dominated by bot/email-security-gateway URL scanners that inflate pageviews with zero clicks (3,379 sessions, 35s avg, 0.07 CTAs each); (3) keeper-security is the one merchant that may have a real page issue — zero offer-engagement CTAs captured in 14 days, only modal-close + outbound clicks. Possibly the new-platform page has no offer data for that merchant.
📊 Cohort-wide validation — 30 50/50 merchants, last 14 days, by source × device:
Source Device Referrer Sessions Avg session % with CTA
emailDesktop$direct3,37935s5.7% ❌
emailDesktopwww.groupon.com312163s27.6%
emailMobile$direct1,108110s24.0%
non-emailDesktopwww.groupon.com35,00194s48.2% ✅
non-emailMobilewww.groupon.com67,93054s41.7%
The email-Desktop $direct row (3,379 sessions, 35s, 5.7% CTA) is the textbook fingerprint of email-security-gateway URL scanners (Microsoft Defender / Proofpoint / Mimecast). They land with utm_medium=email but don't render the email — referrer is $direct, no clicks, ~30s timeout. When email-Desktop has a real www.groupon.com referrer (real human clicks from webmail context), CTR jumps to 27.6% — much closer to a normal cohort.
🔴 5 of 6 chronic merchants are likely false positives
  • 1-800 Contacts, Kay Jewelers, Chime, Zales, Sixt Car Rental: direct-traffic CTR 45-79% on new platform — pages work fine for non-email visitors. Aggregate dragged down by email-blast cohorts (CTR 0-20%).
  • Pattern across all 5: a single-day or recurring email blast (60-185 pv) drove most of the 7-day volume; CTAs and modal flows function correctly on direct/google/chatgpt traffic.
  • keeper-security is the one possible real bug — over 14 days / 162 PVs, only modal-close + outbound clicks captured. Zero offer-engagement CTAs (Get Code/Get Deal/Get Reward) in the event stream. Could be: empty offer state for new-platform routing, blocking modal, or page template mismatch. Worth eng look.
  • Action: file 1 keeper-security bug + 1 spike (email-flow / bot-traffic measurement). Don't file 5 P0 tickets for the false positives.
🤖 Email-Desktop $direct is bot/scanner traffic
  • 3,379 sessions over 14 days, 35s avg duration, 0.07 CTAs each, $direct referrer.
  • Profile matches enterprise email-security URL scanners — they pre-scan all links before delivery to detect phishing.
  • Inflates "email" pageview volume materially while contributing zero engagement. Pulls aggregate email CTR down even when real-human email CTR is healthy.
  • Action: add bot-filtering to PostHog (sessions with $direct + Desktop + utm_medium=email + 0 clicks + <60s should be flagged); ask Veronika to consider similar filter on Tableau.
📧 Real-email CTR is still lower than direct (but not catastrophically)
  • Email-Mobile CTR: 27%; non-email-Mobile: 51% — email is ~half conversion of direct on mobile. Not a bug, but a real cohort intent gap.
  • Email-Desktop with real referrer: 28% vs non-email-Desktop 48% — same ~half ratio.
  • Aggregate Tableau CTR is sensitive to email-blast volume. utm-segmented view (Veronika) needed for clean per-merchant signal.
🔴 Direct-only CTR dropped ~10pp Wk1→Wk2
  • 50/50 cohort, direct only: Apr 14-19 ~39-41% → Apr 21-27 ~29-32%. NOT a traffic-mix artifact.
  • Possible drivers: deploy/config change Apr 19-20, link to Apr 17→18 routing drift, content/template tweak.
  • Action: ask Richard about deploys Apr 19-20; pull direct-only Tableau slice.
🟣 PostHog error tracking is empty project-wide
  • error-tracking-issues-list returns 0 issues; no $exception events on any merchant URL. Either error capture isn't wired up or it's filtered out. Turn on for new platform pages.

AI analysis pending — run Full Refresh to generate insights.

Phase 2 Live Status — April 1

3,524 US merchants deployed. Issues being resolved. Updated 11:20 CET.

IssueStatusOwnerImpact
50/50 split not working✅ FixedRichardRelated to Akamai → Cloudflare migration. Fix deployed, 50/50 now working.
Rewards: 50/50 backtab redirect to legacy🟣 QARichardFix deployed. Backtab on 50/50 merchants was redirecting to legacy. Non-offer tile CTAs verified working (shein). Needs QA confirmation.
Rewards CTA not redirecting to merchant✅ FixedRichard"Yes I understand" CTA now redirects correctly.
SEO test pages reverting to legacy🟠 MonitoringRichard / RoutingATT, AliExpress + others. May be related to 50/50 fix — verifying.
50/50 experiment_impression tracking✅ DoneMarcosGTM updated for all 41 merchants. Documented. Awaiting BI validation.
Rich content / backend data update🟠 In Progress — ~1.5h remainingJakub1,500 / 3,450 merchants updated. ~105 merchants per 5 min. Remaining ~1,950 merchants ETA ~13:00 CET.
BI database switchover⚠️ CautionMark / BIDashboards/reports may fail intermittently this week.
Decision criteria for Phase 3: Increase traffic allocation from 33% to 66%+ if after 1–2 weeks: (1) 50/50 A/B shows CTR neutral or better, (2) aggregate long-tail CTR does not drop >10% vs legacy-protected cohort, (3) no revenue per session degradation. Rollback trigger: significant CTR or revenue drop correlated with platform change across multiple cohorts.

Relative change from each cohort's own pre-rollout average. Source: BQ rep.12_offer_dashboard. Phase 2 data (Apr 1+) will appear in next refresh.

CTR = Clicks / Unique Views · RPV = Commission / Unique Views (cents) · Traffic = Unique Views change. Phase 1 RPV n/a (no commission tracked for those merchants in this table).

CTR by Cohort — Daily Trend

4 cohorts: Legacy Protected (control), 50/50 A/B, 33% Split, Phase 1. Red dashed line = Phase 1 rollout (Mar 18). Phase 2 data (Apr 1+) in next refresh.

Revenue per Unique View (RPV) by Cohort

Commission / Unique Views (cents). Catches revenue degradation even when CTR looks stable. Phase 1 excluded (no commission data).

Daily Unique Views by Cohort

Absolute traffic volume per cohort. Declining traffic in any cohort may indicate SEO or routing issues.

Daily Commission by Cohort

Commission ($) per cohort per day. Stacked view shows total revenue contribution. Phase 1 excluded (near-zero commission).

Rollout Strategy

We're migrating US coupon merchant pages from legacy to a new platform in controlled phases, validating performance at each stage before increasing exposure.

POC
Jan – Mar 14
4 merchants on 100% new platform. Validated core functionality, page rendering, and caching. Confirmed no critical issues — platform ready for broader testing.
Phase 1
Mar 18
48 → 54 merchants — started with 48 (38 at 100% new, 10 at 50/50 split), expanded to 54. First controlled performance measurement. Results: aggregate CTR stable, site speed improvements confirmed. ✓ Completed
Phase 2
Apr 1 — NOW
3,524 merchants — expanded to all US merchants with conservative traffic allocation:
• 407 merchants kept on 100% legacy (high-revenue + SEO-critical protection)
• 41 merchants on 50/50 A/B split (top revenue — controlled experiment)
• ~3,033 merchants receive 33% traffic on new platform, 67% on legacy
• Phase 1 merchants continue unchanged
✓ Deployed — monitoring for CTR, revenue, and error signals.
Phase 3
TBD
Increase traffic allocation — if Phase 2 signals are positive (CTR neutral or better, no revenue impact), increase the 33% split to 66% or 100% for the long-tail merchants. Gradually reduce the legacy-protected bucket. Decision based on 1–2 weeks of Phase 2 data.
Measurement Plan — DMMM Framework
Goal
Validate new platform doesn't degrade CTR before scaling to 100% of US merchants
Tactics
Phased rollout: 4 → 54 → 3,524 merchants. 50/50 experiments on top 30 revenue merchants. Legacy protection for 407 high-value merchants.
KPIs
CTR (primary), CWV (LCP, INP, CLS), bounce rate, error rates, SEO rankings
Targets
CTR neutral or positive vs legacy baseline (same merchant, matched period). CWV: maintain "Passed" status.
Segments
(1) Code Position 1: has P1 code vs no P1 code
(2) Content Quality: quality offers vs spam/filler
(3) Traffic Allocation: 100% / 50-50 / 33-67 / legacy
(4) Revenue Tier

Framework: Avinash Kaushik's Digital Marketing & Measurement Model (DMMM)

What We're Measuring

Platform Performance Test (50/50 Split)

Top 30 revenue merchants run 50/50 legacy vs new platform. This is the controlled experiment — same merchants, same time, same offers. Controls for seasonality and offer quality.
This is the definitive signal for platform impact on CTR and revenue.

Gradual Rollout (33% Traffic Split)

~3,033 remaining merchants receive 33% of traffic on new platform, 67% on legacy. The 407 legacy-protected merchants serve as a control group for aggregate comparison.
Compare aggregate CTR of 33% cohort vs legacy-protected cohort to detect platform-level issues.
Decision criteria for Phase 3: Increase traffic allocation from 33% to 66%+ if after 1–2 weeks: (1) 50/50 A/B shows CTR neutral or better, (2) aggregate long-tail CTR does not drop >10% vs legacy-protected cohort, (3) no revenue per session degradation. Rollback trigger: significant CTR or revenue drop correlated with platform change across multiple cohorts.

BI recommends switching to unique views — pending data refresh. Individual merchant before/after comparisons are uncontrolled and confounded by offer quality changes. Aggregate and 50/50 split comparisons are more reliable.

• Top 30 Revenue (50/50): 50/50 A/B split merchants
• Phase 1 merchants: Original rollout merchants — do not rename tab (BQ auto-feed)
